macro_context.headline: Trump rejects Iran peace proposal over Strait of Hormuz demands; oil surges past $99, US CPI Tuesday looms as next catalyst.

EURUSD.name: Euro / US Dollar
EURUSD.past: EUR edged to 1.1769 as the dollar softened on risk-on flows into Asian equities and tech. DXY held below 98.10 for a third straight session.
EURUSD.why: Weak dollar momentum is structural — markets are pricing two Fed cuts by September while ECB stays hawkish on sticky services inflation.
EURUSD.watch: US CPI Tuesday 15:30 TRT is the week's pivot. Core above 0.3% MoM would reprice Fed path and reverse dollar weakness sharply.

headlines.title: Dollar slides as risk appetite returns on Asian tech rally

GBPUSD.name: British Pound / US Dollar
GBPUSD.past: Cable gained 45 pips to 1.3601, supported by broad dollar weakness and solid UK services PMI data last week.
GBPUSD.why: BoE rate path divergence from the Fed is widening — UK inflation persistence keeps the BoE on hold while Fed signals cuts. Sterling benefits.
GBPUSD.watch: UK employment data Wednesday 09:00 TRT. Rising wages would reinforce BoE hawkishness and push cable toward 1.3700.

headlines.title: Sterling gains as BoE holds firm amid persistent inflation

USDJPY.name: US Dollar / Japanese Yen
USDJPY.past: Dollar-yen crept higher to 157.03 despite broad dollar weakness — yen underperformed as risk appetite sent capital out of safe havens.
USDJPY.why: BoJ's July rate hike expectations are fading after soft Tokyo CPI. The carry trade is back on, capping yen strength even as DXY weakens.
USDJPY.watch: Japan GDP Wednesday 02:50 TRT. Contraction would kill remaining BoJ hike bets and open 158+ quickly.

headlines.title: Yen weakens as risk appetite overrides safe-haven demand

AUDUSD.name: Australian Dollar / US Dollar
AUDUSD.past: Aussie rallied to 0.7242, within 0.5% of its 52-week high. Commodities strength and Asian tech optimism lifted the risk-sensitive currency.
AUDUSD.why: China stimulus expectations plus surging commodity prices create a perfect storm for AUD. Iron ore above $140 adds fundamental support.
AUDUSD.watch: RBA minutes Tuesday 03:30 TRT. Any hint of hawkish tilt at the next meeting could push AUD through the 52-week high at 0.7277.

headlines.title: Australian dollar rallies to near 52-week high on commodities boom

XAUUSD.name: Gold
XAUUSD.past: Gold dropped $35 to $4,684 despite Trump rejecting Iran's peace deal. Risk-on flows into equities and crypto pulled capital away from the safe haven.
XAUUSD.why: Gold's failure to rally on geopolitical escalation is a bearish signal. The metal is trading as a crowded long, not a hedge — profit-taking dominates.
XAUUSD.watch: US CPI Tuesday is the swing factor. Hot print revives real-rate fears and pressures gold further; soft print restores $4,730+.

headlines.title: Gold falls despite Iran tensions as risk appetite holds

XAGUSD.name: Silver
XAGUSD.past: Silver gained nearly 1% to $81.15, outperforming gold. Industrial demand narrative continues to dominate the gold-silver ratio compression.
XAGUSD.why: Silver's dual role — industrial metal plus monetary hedge — makes it the better play in a risk-on, inflation-worried environment. Solar panel demand keeps a floor under prices.
XAGUSD.watch: Gold-silver ratio near 57.7 — if gold breaks below $4,650, silver follows regardless of industrial demand. Watch $79.50 support.

headlines.title: Silver outperforms gold on industrial demand tailwinds

USOIL.name: WTI Crude Oil
USOIL.past: WTI surged 3.75% to $99.00 after Trump rejected Iran's peace offer over Strait of Hormuz demands. Brent pushed above $100.
USOIL.why: The failed diplomacy reignites supply disruption fears. Iran exports 1.5M bbl/day — any escalation threatens the already-tight global supply balance with OPEC+ slow to add barrels.
USOIL.watch: $100 psychological resistance is the line. Break above opens $103-$105 range. De-escalation headline could slam it back to $95.

headlines.title: Oil surges past $99 as Trump rejects Iran peace deal

US500.name: S&P 500
US500.past: S&P closed at 7,398.9, within 3 points of its all-time high. Tech and semis led the charge as AI capex optimism overrode Iran headlines.
US500.why: The market is pricing peak earnings growth — AI spending is accelerating faster than even bulls expected. Breadth is narrowing though, which historically precedes corrections.
US500.watch: Intraday ATH test at 7,401.5 is imminent. Clean break targets 7,500. CPI miss Tuesday could trigger the breakout; hot CPI kills it.

headlines.title: S&P 500 flirts with all-time high as AI trade dominates

NAS100.name: Nasdaq 100
NAS100.past: Nasdaq 100 hit a new all-time high at 29,235 — a 2.35% surge led by semis after the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index also reached a record.
NAS100.why: AI capex cycle is entering a new phase. Hyperscalers are spending faster than consensus, and the market is rewarding the entire supply chain — from chips to cloud to cooling.
NAS100.watch: 30,000 round number is the next magnet. Earnings from Cisco and Applied Materials this week will test whether the AI narrative has more room.

headlines.title: Nasdaq hits record as Asian chipmakers surge on AI demand

US30.name: Dow Jones Industrial Average
US30.past: Dow barely moved, adding just 12 points to 49,609. Old-economy names lagged as money rotated hard into growth and semis.
US30.why: The Dow's flatness while Nasdaq surges 2.35% signals a classic rotation. Industrials and financials need rate-cut clarity that CPI data could provide.
US30.watch: 50,000 psychological level remains the target. Needs breadth expansion from industrials and financials — unlikely without softer inflation data.

headlines.title: Dow lags as rotation into tech accelerates

BTC.name: Bitcoin
BTC.past: Bitcoin slipped 1.7% to $80,707 as weekend liquidity thinned out. Failed to hold $82,000 support after a brief rally attempt.
BTC.why: BTC is stuck in no-man's land — too far from ATH to attract momentum buyers, too high for value accumulation. Equities are stealing the risk appetite.
BTC.watch: $79,000 is the line. Break below opens $77,500 and potentially $75,000. ETF flow data Monday will signal institutional conviction.

headlines.title: Bitcoin dips below $81K as weekend selling pressure builds

ETH.name: Ethereum
ETH.past: ETH dropped 1.7% to $2,330, underperforming BTC again. Solana developer share jumped to 23% at Ethereum's expense per a new ecosystem report.
ETH.why: Ethereum's developer exodus narrative is weighing on sentiment. ETH/BTC ratio keeps grinding lower as capital flows to faster L1s and BTC ETFs.
ETH.watch: $2,280 support is critical — it's held three times since April. Break below targets $2,200. Pectra upgrade timeline updates could shift sentiment.

headlines.title: Solana developer share jumps to 23% as Ethereum weakens

NVDA.name: NVIDIA
NVDA.past: NVIDIA gained 1.75% to $215.20, within 1.2% of its all-time high. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index hit a record, lifting the entire sector.
NVDA.why: AI capex acceleration is the strongest tailwind in markets right now. Every hyperscaler is spending more than guided, and NVIDIA captures the lion's share of GPU demand.
NVDA.watch: ATH at $217.80 is the near-term test. Earnings May 28 loom large — any guidance miss after this run could trigger a 10%+ pullback.

headlines.title: NVIDIA approaches all-time high as semiconductor index hits record

TSLA.name: Tesla
TSLA.past: Tesla surged 4% to $428.35, its best day in three weeks. Wedbush reiterated a bull case tied to robotaxi timeline acceleration.
TSLA.why: The stock trades on narrative, not fundamentals. Robotaxi and energy storage are the two catalysts bulls are pricing in. China sales data remains the bear counterpoint.
TSLA.watch: $440 resistance from the April high is the next hurdle. Cybertruck recall headlines could cap momentum if they resurface.

headlines.title: Wedbush sends blunt message on Elon Musk and Tesla's future

AAPL.name: Apple
AAPL.past: Apple rallied 2% to $293.32, just $1.44 from its all-time high. The 2026 chip war narrative — with Apple dominating custom silicon — fueled the bid.
AAPL.why: Apple's vertical integration moat is finally being priced as an AI advantage. On-device AI processing via Apple Silicon gives it a unique privacy-first positioning.
AAPL.watch: ATH at $294.76 is one strong session away. WWDC in June with new AI features is the next major catalyst. Break above $295 targets $300.

headlines.title: The 2026 chip war is already over and Apple dominated

MSFT.name: Microsoft
MSFT.past: Microsoft fell 1.3% to $415.12 as reports surfaced about its troubled African data center expansion. The stock has lagged mega-cap peers all year.
MSFT.why: MSFT is the odd one out in the AI rally — 25% off highs while peers flirt with records. Azure growth deceleration and heavy capex spending are eroding the bull case.
MSFT.watch: $410 support has held since April. Break below exposes $400. Needs a positive Azure growth data point to rejoin the AI rally.

headlines.title: Microsoft African data center expansion falters

META.name: Meta Platforms
META.past: Meta slipped 1.2% to $609.63 as broader tech rotation favored hardware/semis over software/advertising names. Still 23% off its high.
META.why: Meta's $3.3B EU fine and ongoing Reality Labs losses are headwinds. The market is rewarding AI infrastructure plays over AI application plays this cycle.
META.watch: $600 is the psychological floor. Trump's landmark social media regulation could create additional regulatory overhang if it advances.

headlines.title: Meta got $3.3 billion EU fine over data practices

GOOGL.name: Alphabet
GOOGL.past: Alphabet rose 0.7% to $400.80, within $1.20 of its all-time high. Analysts flagged it as the best-positioned mega-cap for AI monetization.
GOOGL.why: GOOGL is the stealth AI winner. Search + Cloud + Waymo gives it the broadest AI moat. The stock has quietly tripled from its 52-week low.
GOOGL.watch: ATH at $402 is the immediate test. Clean break with volume targets $410-$420. Google I/O later this month is the next narrative event.

headlines.title: AI wins: Alphabet poised to become top beneficiary

AMZN.name: Amazon
AMZN.past: Amazon gained 0.6% to $272.68. AWS growth reacceleration and the expanding AI moat narrative kept the stock within 2% of highs.
AMZN.why: Amazon's custom Trainium chips and AWS's AI inference platform are positioning it as the picks-and-shovels play alongside NVIDIA. Margins are expanding.
AMZN.watch: 52-week high at $278.56 is the target. Prime Day timing announcement and any new AWS AI product launches could catalyze the breakout.

headlines.title: Amazon's quiet AI moat keeps expanding
